mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rate above 5.0% signals monetary tightening that increases discount rates on future corporate earnings, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 5.26% on 2007-02-01 indicates active tightening cycle
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 5.0%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window during tightening regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate below 4.7% signals tight labor market conditions that increase wage pressures and inflation expectations, forcing the Fed to maintain restrictive policy and constraining equity returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.5% on 2007-02-01 indicates tight labor market
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of triggered instances, or correlation between UNRATE and subsequent 60-day returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Declining housing starts below 1,400 thousand units signals weakening residential investment demand, which cascades into reduced consumer wealth effects and lower aggregate demand, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as forward-looking equity markets price in demand contraction.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1408.0 thousand units on 2007-01-01 is near critical threshold
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in > 58% of instances when HOUST < 1400, or Spearman rank correlation between HOUST and 5-day returns is > -0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth decelerates below 1.5% year-over-year, indicating weakening manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side constraints that reduce corporate profit margins, driving negative equity returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 111.9282 on 2007-01-01 shows supply-side activity level
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO momentum and 20-day forward returns is < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or direction of relationship reverses in > 40% of sub-periods.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales and federal funds rate interact such that when RSAFS growth decelerates below 3% year-over-year while FEDFUNDS remains above 5.0%, the combination signals demand destruction under monetary constraint, producing negative 20-day forward returns. The interaction captures the regime where monetary tightening bites into consumer spending.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 370495.0M and FEDFUNDS at 5.26% on 2007-02-01 satisfy joint condition
failure_condition: When both conditions are met, SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 52% of instances, or the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression of forward return direction.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth decelerates below 100 thousand persons per month while unemployment rate rises above 4.6%, signaling labor market deterioration that reduces consumer confidence and discretionary spending, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as equity markets price in demand contraction.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 137410.0 thousand and UNRATE at 4.5% on 2007-02-01
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in > 58% of instances when both conditions are met, or Rank IC of the interaction term is < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation measured by CPIAUCSL rises above 2.5% year-over-year while FEDFUNDS remains below 5.5%, indicating real rates are insufficiently restrictive to contain inflation expectations. This regime mismatch forces future Fed tightening, producing negative 60-day forward returns as markets anticipate policy normalization.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 203.153 on 2007-01-01 and FEDFUNDS at 5.26% on 2007-02-01
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression controlling for individual series effects.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth decelerates below 2.0% annualized while housing starts fall below 1,350 thousand units, signaling synchronized contraction in both aggregate demand and residential investment. This dual weakness produces negative 60-day forward returns as equity markets price in recession risk.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 11506.5B and HOUST at 1408.0K on 2007-01-01
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in > 58% of instances when both conditions are met, or Spearman rank correlation of the interaction signal with forward returns is < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.
